assertions.text: The vehicle was stationary (not moving) immediately before the collision.
There was a single impact during the collision (one distinct strike).
A heavy impact struck the car (the car was struck by another object rather than striking something).
The impact was heavy (greater than a minor/light tap).
The vehicle was stationary and remained stationary until it was struck (no motion occurred between being stationary and the impact).

verdicts.evidence: The GPS speed was nonzero before the impact: [GPS speed first_value 0.0 km/h at t=0.0 and later values rising to a max of 47.0 km/h](T2), and detect_events reports a speed drop from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0](T3), so the vehicle was not stationary immediately prior to the collision.

Detect_events shows a single primary impact spike on accel_x at [peak_t 11.01 s with magnitude 5.86 g](T3) and an impact spike on accel_y at [peak_t 11.01 s with magnitude 2.67 g](T3) that coincide in time, indicating one distinct collision event (single impact spike window from [t_start 10.99 to t_end 11.02 s](T3)).

Sensors show a strong impact impulse on device axes ([accel_x peak_signed_dev -5.862 g at t=11.01](T2) and [accel_z peak_raw_value 4.825 g at t=11.03](T2)), but device-frame axis alignment to vehicle-frame is undocumented so we cannot determine from sensors alone whether the car was struck by another object versus striking something; axis direction relative to vehicle is unverifiable.

The IMU recorded a large impact spike with peak magnitude [5.86 g on accel_x at t=11.01 s](T3) which exceeds the impact-spike threshold and indicates a heavy impact rather than a minor tap; accel_z also showed a [peak deviation of 3.816 g at t=11.03 s](T2).

GPS speed evidence shows the vehicle was moving before the impact: detect_events reports a speed drop from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0](T3), and compute_stats shows gps_speed last_value [2.0 km/h at t=16.0 but max 47.0 km/h in window](T2); thus the vehicle was not stationary and did not remain stationary up to impact.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a single strong impact spike (supported) but contradicts the claim the vehicle was stationary before impact (GPS speed and speed-drop detections show substantial speed prior to impact). Axis alignment is undocumented so impact direction (struck vs striking) is unverifiable.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
